titel: Certain adjustments to the income tax law have been made due to changes in compensation for newly arrived immigrants.
html:
The proposal includes suggested amendments to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229). It is proposed that the new benefit, the "child allowance," be tax-free, and that the "establishment allowance" and "housing allowance" be removed from the law.

The proposed changes are scheduled to take effect on September 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:189

Government's proposal: The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Income Tax Act (1999:1299). 3

Prop. 2025/26:189 2 Proposal for an amendment to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229)

Hereby it is prescribed that section 8, paragraph 13 of the Income Tax Act (1999:1229) 1 shall have the following wording.

Current wording Proposed wording

8. 13 § 2 Establishment allowance, establishment and child allowance, and housing allowance for certain newly arrived immigrants are tax-free. Establishment allowance and child allowance for certain newly arrived immigrants are tax-free.

  1. This law shall enter into force on September 1, 2026.
  2. Previous regulations shall continue to apply to establishment allowances and housing allowances paid out after the entry into force.

4 1 The law is reprinted 2008:803.
